docs.text: Kyro A lightweight language written in Rust Kyro is a lightweight, toy programming language built in Rust, inspired by the Lox language from Crafting Interpreters . It features a tree-walk interpreter architecture with modern enhancements such as a static resolution pass, compiled string interpolation, and a namespace-isolated standard library. The language is designed for simplicity and extensibility, supporting object-oriented programming (OOP), structured exception handling, and first-class collection types like lists and dictionaries
docs.title: Home

docs.text: A lightweight language written in Rust Kyro is a lightweight, toy programming language built in Rust, inspired by the Lox language from Crafting Interpreters . It features a tree-walk interpreter architecture with modern enhancements such as a static resolution pass, compiled string interpolation, and a namespace-isolated standard library. The language is designed for simplicity and extensibility, supporting object-oriented programming (OOP), structured exception handling, and first-class collection types like lists and dictionaries
docs.title: Kyro

docs.text: The core global functions, reflection utilities, and built-in literal types available in kyro . Also look at: std : built-in standard library (native in rust). lib : modules under lib/ written in kyro. Built-in Types Lists ( [...] ) Lists are ordered, mutable collections of values. * Internal Representation: Value::List variant wrapping a heap-allocated Rc<RefCell<Vec<Value>>> . Syntax & Manipulation // Literal construction var list = [ 1 , 2 , 3 ]; // Subscript indexing var first = list [ 0 ]; // Index assignment list [ 1 ] = "mutated" ; Bound Methods .len() : Returns the number of elements in the list. .push(val) : Appends a value to the end of the list. .pop() : Removes and returns the last element of the list. Dictionaries ( {...} ) Dictionaries are mutable key-value maps. * Internal Representation: Value::Dict variant wrapping an Rc<RefCell<HashMap<String, Value>>> . Syntax & Manipulation // Literal construction var dict = { "key" : "val" }; // Subscript indexing var value = dict [ "key" ]; // Index assignment dict [ "new_key" ] = 42 ; Bound Methods .len() : Returns the number of key-value pairs. .keys() : Returns a list of all keys in the dictionary. .remove(key) : Removes the specified key and returns its associated value. Strings Strings represent UTF-8 encoded text sequences. * Internal Representation: Value::String variant. Bound Methods .len() : Returns the character length of the string. .slice(start, end) : Returns a substring from the start index up to (but excluding) the end index. .split(separator) : Splits the string into a list of substrings based on the provided separator. Numbers Numbers represent numeric values in Kyro. * Internal Representation: Value::Number variant. Bound Methods .floor() : Rounds the number down to the nearest integer. .ceil() : Rounds the number up to the nearest integer. .round() : Rounds the number to the nearest integer. .abs() : Returns the absolute value of the number. .to_string() : Converts the numeric value to its string representation. Core Globals & Reflection id(item) Returns the unique memory address of the given item. * Backend Implementation: IdFn Rust struct. Usage: var x = [ 1 , 2 , 3 ]; print ( id ( x )); // Prints the memory address of the list dir(item) Returns a list of strings containing all keys, methods, and attributes associated with the specified item. * Backend Implementation: DirFn Rust struct. Usage: var list = [ 1 , 2 ]; print ( dir ( list )); // ["len", "push", "pop", ...] use(module_name) Loads a standard library module. All standard library modules in Kyro are namespace-isolated and must be explicitly imported. Calling use returns a module instance containing its native functions and values. Backend Implementation: Use Rust struct. Returns a KyroInstance wrapping a dynamic KyroClass whose fields point to native KyroCallable structs. Usage: var math = use ( "math" ); print ( math . floor ( 3.14 )); __name__ A pre-loaded global variable storing the current module's name. For the primary entrypoint script, this evaluates to the string "__main__" . Usage: if __name__ == "__main__" { print ( "Running as main script" ); } instance.__class__ Accessing __class__ on any class instance returns its underlying class definition as a class object. This allows you to inspect class metadata dynamically. Usage: // instance.__class__.__name__ evaluates to the PascalCase class name var class_name = my_instance . __class__ . __name__ ; callable.__name__ Accessing __name__ on any callable object (such as a class or function) returns its string identifier. Usage: print ( my_function . __name__ ); // "my_function"
docs.title: core

docs.text: Loads a standard library module. All standard library modules in Kyro are namespace-isolated and must be explicitly imported. Calling use returns a module instance containing its native functions and values. Backend Implementation: Use Rust struct. Returns a KyroInstance wrapping a dynamic KyroClass whose fields point to native KyroCallable structs. Usage: var math = use ( "math" ); print ( math . floor ( 3.14 ));
docs.title: use(module_name)

docs.text: kyro can currently be installed only through source. Basic knowledge about using a terminal is expected. To install, follow these steps: Prerequisites Rust for compiling the binary: https://rust-lang.org/tools/install/ Git (Optional) to clone the repo: https://git-scm.com/ , alternatively, download it manually from github. Clone the repository locally git clone https://github.com/externref/kyro cd kyro Make the script executable in the shell Make sure you're in the repo's directory and run the following command linux/macos chmod +x install.sh windows Set-ExecutionPolicy -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -Scope Process Build and setup the language binary (and library) linux/macos ./install.sh windows .\ install . ps1 Note: What does these commands do? both scripts run cargo build -r to compile the language binary. create a .kyro directory in the home folder of the user. move the binary in target/release/ and the lib directory to the .kyro directory. add the directory as KYRO_HOME environment variable and add it to PATH for binary access systemwide. Test Create a test file with any name, for this example we'll use main.kyro var io = use ( "std:io" ); fn main (){ io . println ( "hello, world!" ); } main (); Run this command kyro main.kyro Expected Output: externref @MSI : ~/ projects / kyro $ kyro main . kyro hello , world ! externref @MSI : ~/ projects / kyro $
docs.title: Installation

docs.text: Rust for compiling the binary: https://rust-lang.org/tools/install/ Git (Optional) to clone the repo: https://git-scm.com/ , alternatively, download it manually from github.

docs.text: linux/macos ./install.sh windows .\ install . ps1 Note: What does these commands do? both scripts run cargo build -r to compile the language binary. create a .kyro directory in the home folder of the user. move the binary in target/release/ and the lib directory to the .kyro directory. add the directory as KYRO_HOME environment variable and add it to PATH for binary access systemwide.
docs.title: Build and setup the language binary (and library)

docs.text: Create a test file with any name, for this example we'll use main.kyro var io = use ( "std:io" ); fn main (){ io . println ( "hello, world!" ); } main (); Run this command kyro main.kyro Expected Output: externref @MSI : ~/ projects / kyro $ kyro main . kyro hello , world ! externref @MSI : ~/ projects / kyro $
docs.title: Test

docs.text: The language uses a C-style syntax with modern language features, including function declarations with fn , class -based OOP with inheritance using < , and structured exception handling with try/catch/throw . Declarations Variables Variables are declared with the var keyword and can be optionally initialized. Uninitialized variables default to nil . var x = 10 ; var uninitialized ; Functions Functions are defined using the fn keyword, followed by a name, parameter list in parentheses, and a body block. fn multiply ( x , y ) { return x * y ; } multiply ( 2 , 3 ) // 6 Classes Classes are declared with the class keyword and can optionally inherit from a parent class using < . Methods are defined within the class body. var io = use ( "std:io" ); class Animal { do_sound (){ io . println ( "generic animal sound" ); } } class Cat < Animal { init ( name ){ this . name = name ; } do_sound (){ io . println ( "${this.name} says meow" ); } } fn main (){ var cat = Cat ( "bob" ); cat . do_sound (); } Statements Control Flow Kyro supports standard C-style control flow structures with conditions enclosed in parentheses. // if/else if ( x > 10 ) { echo "greater" ; } else { echo "less or equal" ; } // while loop while ( x > 0 ) { x = x - 1 ; } // for loop for ( var i = 0 ; i < 5 ; i = i + 1 ) { echo i ; } Exception Handling Exceptions are handled with try / catch blocks, and can be thrown with any value type. try { var value = list [ 10 ]; } catch ( err ) { echo "failed: " + err ; } throw "something went wrong" ; Output The echo statement prints values followed by a newline. echo "hello world" ; Expressions Literals and Collections Kyro supports lists, dictionaries, and string interpolation with compile-time parsing. // lists var fruits = [ "apple" , "banana" ]; // dictionaries var user = { "name" : "bob" , "role" : "dev" }; // string interpolation var label = "progress" ; var percent = 50 ; echo "${label}: ${percent}%" ; // progress: 50% Operators The language uses standard C-style operators for arithmetic, comparison, and logic. For a quick overview on the supported keywords here's the TokenType enum. #[rustfmt::skip] #[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)] pub enum TokenType { LeftParen , RightParen , LeftBrace , RightBrace , LeftBracket , RightBracket , Colon , Comma , Dot , Minus , Plus , Semicolon , Slash , Star , Bang , BangEqual , Equal , EqualEqual , Greater , GreaterEqual , Less , LessEqual , Identifier , String , Number , And , Class , Else , False , Fn , For , If , Nil , Or , Echo , Return , Super , This , True , Var , While , Try , Catch , Throw , Break , Continue , Eof , }
docs.title: Syntax

docs.text: Variables are declared with the var keyword and can be optionally initialized. Uninitialized variables default to nil . var x = 10 ; var uninitialized ;
docs.title: Variables

docs.text: Functions are defined using the fn keyword, followed by a name, parameter list in parentheses, and a body block. fn multiply ( x , y ) { return x * y ; } multiply ( 2 , 3 ) // 6
docs.title: Functions

docs.text: Classes are declared with the class keyword and can optionally inherit from a parent class using < . Methods are defined within the class body. var io = use ( "std:io" ); class Animal { do_sound (){ io . println ( "generic animal sound" ); } } class Cat < Animal { init ( name ){ this . name = name ; } do_sound (){ io . println ( "${this.name} says meow" ); } } fn main (){ var cat = Cat ( "bob" ); cat . do_sound (); }
docs.title: Classes

docs.text: Kyro supports standard C-style control flow structures with conditions enclosed in parentheses. // if/else if ( x > 10 ) { echo "greater" ; } else { echo "less or equal" ; } // while loop while ( x > 0 ) { x = x - 1 ; } // for loop for ( var i = 0 ; i < 5 ; i = i + 1 ) { echo i ; }
docs.title: Control Flow

docs.text: Exceptions are handled with try / catch blocks, and can be thrown with any value type. try { var value = list [ 10 ]; } catch ( err ) { echo "failed: " + err ; } throw "something went wrong" ;
docs.title: Exception Handling

docs.text: The echo statement prints values followed by a newline. echo "hello world" ;
docs.title: Output

docs.text: Kyro supports lists, dictionaries, and string interpolation with compile-time parsing. // lists var fruits = [ "apple" , "banana" ]; // dictionaries var user = { "name" : "bob" , "role" : "dev" }; // string interpolation var label = "progress" ; var percent = 50 ; echo "${label}: ${percent}%" ; // progress: 50%
docs.title: Literals and Collections

docs.text: The language uses standard C-style operators for arithmetic, comparison, and logic. For a quick overview on the supported keywords here's the TokenType enum. #[rustfmt::skip] #[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)] pub enum TokenType { LeftParen , RightParen , LeftBrace , RightBrace , LeftBracket , RightBracket , Colon , Comma , Dot , Minus , Plus , Semicolon , Slash , Star , Bang , BangEqual , Equal , EqualEqual , Greater , GreaterEqual , Less , LessEqual , Identifier , String , Number , And , Class , Else , False , Fn , For , If , Nil , Or , Echo , Return , Super , This , True , Var , While , Try , Catch , Throw , Break , Continue , Eof , }

docs.text: library modules The lib module group consists of library files written in Kyro itself, located inside the $KYRO_HOME/lib directory. These can be imported into your scripts using the use function with a lib:<module name> prefix and stored in a namespace. Example var math = use ( "lib:math" ); Quick Note: You can view a module's public functions and variables at runtime using the dir function. var json = use ( "lib:json" ); print ( dir ( json )); index of lib modules lib:json : module for encoding and decoding JSON data structures. lib:math : mathematical constants and utility functions written in kyro.
docs.title: lib modules

docs.text: The lib module group consists of library files written in Kyro itself, located inside the $KYRO_HOME/lib directory. These can be imported into your scripts using the use function with a lib:<module name> prefix and stored in a namespace. Example var math = use ( "lib:math" ); Quick Note: You can view a module's public functions and variables at runtime using the dir function. var json = use ( "lib:json" ); print ( dir ( json ));
docs.title: library modules

docs.text: lib:json : module for encoding and decoding JSON data structures. lib:math : mathematical constants and utility functions written in kyro.
docs.title: index of lib modules

docs.text: The lib:json library module provides utility functions to encode kyro data structures into JSON strings and decode JSON strings back into native kyro objects (such as dictionaries, lists, strings, numbers, booleans, and nil ). To use this module, load it using the lib prefix: var json = use ( "lib:json" ); loads loads ( json_str : String ) -> Any Parses a JSON-encoded string and returns its representation as a native kyro value. Parameters: json_str (String) : The JSON data to parse. Returns: (Any) A kyro dictionary, list, string, number, boolean, or nil . var json = use ( "lib:json" ); var data = json . loads ( "{ \" name \" : \" kyro \" , \" version \" : 1.0, \" active \" : true}" ); print ( data [ "name" ]); // "kyro" print ( data [ "version" ]); // 1.0 print ( data [ "active" ]); // true dumps dumps ( value : Any , indent : Number | Nil ) -> String Serializes a kyro value into a JSON-formatted string. Parameters: value (Any) : The kyro value to serialize. indent (Number | Nil) : The number of spaces to use for indentation. If set to nil , the output will be compact and printed on a single line. Returns: (String) The serialized JSON string. var json = use ( "lib:json" ); var config = { "port" : 8080 , "tags" : [ "web" , "production" ] }; // Compact serialization var compact = json . dumps ( config , nil ); print ( compact ); // {"port": 8080, "tags": ["web", "production"]} // Pretty-printed serialization with 4 spaces of indentation var pretty = json . dumps ( config , 4 ); print ( pretty ); load load ( filepath : String ) -> Any Reads a file from the local file system and parses its contents as JSON. Parameters: filepath (String) : The path to the JSON file. Returns: (Any) The parsed kyro value representing the JSON file's contents. var json = use ( "lib:json" ); var config = json . load ( "config.json" ); print ( config [ "port" ]); dump dump ( value : Any , filepath : String , indent : Number | Nil ) -> Nil Serializes a kyro value into a JSON string and writes it directly to a file at the specified path. Parameters: value (Any) : The kyro value to serialize and write. filepath (String) : The path to the output destination file. indent (Number | Nil) : The indentation level for formatting (or nil for compact output). Returns: (Nil) var json = use ( "lib:json" ); var settings = { "theme" : "dark" , "fontSize" : 14 }; // Writes the settings dictionary to settings.json with 2-space indentation json . dump ( settings , "settings.json" , 2 );
docs.title: json

docs.text: loads ( json_str : String ) -> Any Parses a JSON-encoded string and returns its representation as a native kyro value. Parameters: json_str (String) : The JSON data to parse. Returns: (Any) A kyro dictionary, list, string, number, boolean, or nil . var json = use ( "lib:json" ); var data = json . loads ( "{ \" name \" : \" kyro \" , \" version \" : 1.0, \" active \" : true}" ); print ( data [ "name" ]); // "kyro" print ( data [ "version" ]); // 1.0 print ( data [ "active" ]); // true
docs.title: loads

docs.text: dumps ( value : Any , indent : Number | Nil ) -> String Serializes a kyro value into a JSON-formatted string. Parameters: value (Any) : The kyro value to serialize. indent (Number | Nil) : The number of spaces to use for indentation. If set to nil , the output will be compact and printed on a single line. Returns: (String) The serialized JSON string. var json = use ( "lib:json" ); var config = { "port" : 8080 , "tags" : [ "web" , "production" ] }; // Compact serialization var compact = json . dumps ( config , nil ); print ( compact ); // {"port": 8080, "tags": ["web", "production"]} // Pretty-printed serialization with 4 spaces of indentation var pretty = json . dumps ( config , 4 ); print ( pretty );
docs.title: dumps

docs.text: load ( filepath : String ) -> Any Reads a file from the local file system and parses its contents as JSON. Parameters: filepath (String) : The path to the JSON file. Returns: (Any) The parsed kyro value representing the JSON file's contents. var json = use ( "lib:json" ); var config = json . load ( "config.json" ); print ( config [ "port" ]);
docs.title: load

docs.text: dump ( value : Any , filepath : String , indent : Number | Nil ) -> Nil Serializes a kyro value into a JSON string and writes it directly to a file at the specified path. Parameters: value (Any) : The kyro value to serialize and write. filepath (String) : The path to the output destination file. indent (Number | Nil) : The indentation level for formatting (or nil for compact output). Returns: (Nil) var json = use ( "lib:json" ); var settings = { "theme" : "dark" , "fontSize" : 14 }; // Writes the settings dictionary to settings.json with 2-space indentation json . dump ( settings , "settings.json" , 2 );
docs.title: dump

docs.text: This module contains mathematical constants and trigonometric functions written in kyro. Include using var math = use ( "lib:math" ); PI PI : Number The mathematical constant $\pi$ (pi), representing the ratio of the circumference of a circle to its diameter. Value: 3.141592653589793 TWO_PI TWO_PI : Number The mathematical constant $2\pi$ (tau), representing a full turn in radians. Value: 6.283185307179586 sum sum ( args : List ) -> Number Calculates the sum of all numerical elements in a list. Parameters: args (List) : A list of numbers to be summed. Returns: (Number) The total sum of the elements. var math = use ( "lib:math" ); var values = [ 1 , 2 , 3 , 4 , 5 ]; print ( math . sum ( values )); // 15 sin sin ( x : Number ) -> Number Calculates the sine of an angle $x$ (expressed in radians) using a Taylor series approximation. Parameters: x (Number) : The angle in radians. Returns: (Number) The sine of $x$. var math = use ( "lib:math" ); print ( math . sin ( math . PI / 2 )); // 1 cos cos ( x : Number ) -> Number Calculates the cosine of an angle $x$ (expressed in radians) using a Taylor series approximation. Parameters: x (Number) : The angle in radians. Returns: (Number) The cosine of $x$. var math = use ( "lib:math" ); print ( math . cos ( math . PI )); // -1 tan tan ( x : Number ) -> Number Calculates the tangent of an angle $x$ (expressed in radians). Parameters: x (Number) : The angle in radians. Returns: (Number) The tangent of $x$. var math = use ( "lib:math" ); print ( math . tan ( 0 )); // 0
docs.title: math

docs.text: PI : Number The mathematical constant $\pi$ (pi), representing the ratio of the circumference of a circle to its diameter. Value: 3.141592653589793
docs.title: PI

docs.text: TWO_PI : Number The mathematical constant $2\pi$ (tau), representing a full turn in radians. Value: 6.283185307179586
docs.title: TWO_PI

docs.text: sum ( args : List ) -> Number Calculates the sum of all numerical elements in a list. Parameters: args (List) : A list of numbers to be summed. Returns: (Number) The total sum of the elements. var math = use ( "lib:math" ); var values = [ 1 , 2 , 3 , 4 , 5 ]; print ( math . sum ( values )); // 15
docs.title: sum

docs.text: sin ( x : Number ) -> Number Calculates the sine of an angle $x$ (expressed in radians) using a Taylor series approximation. Parameters: x (Number) : The angle in radians. Returns: (Number) The sine of $x$. var math = use ( "lib:math" ); print ( math . sin ( math . PI / 2 )); // 1
docs.title: sin

docs.text: cos ( x : Number ) -> Number Calculates the cosine of an angle $x$ (expressed in radians) using a Taylor series approximation. Parameters: x (Number) : The angle in radians. Returns: (Number) The cosine of $x$. var math = use ( "lib:math" ); print ( math . cos ( math . PI )); // -1
docs.title: cos

docs.text: tan ( x : Number ) -> Number Calculates the tangent of an angle $x$ (expressed in radians). Parameters: x (Number) : The angle in radians. Returns: (Number) The tangent of $x$. var math = use ( "lib:math" ); print ( math . tan ( 0 )); // 0
docs.title: tan

docs.text: built-in modules The std module group consists of built-in modules for the language that are written and constructed natively in rust. These can be imported into your scripts using the use function with a std:<module name> prefix and stored in a namespace. Example var io = use ( "std:io" ); Quick Note: You can view module content using the dir function for undocumented components of the module. var io = use ( "std:io" ); echo dir ( io ); // [__name__, input, print, println] index of std modules std:fs : file system operation related functions and variables. std:io : module with functions for taking inputs and outputs to and from console. std:os : functions and classes handling OS and environment related operations. std:time : module with system clock and time related functions. std:util : utility functions for conversions, shortcuts and language features.
docs.title: std modules

docs.text: The std module group consists of built-in modules for the language that are written and constructed natively in rust. These can be imported into your scripts using the use function with a std:<module name> prefix and stored in a namespace. Example var io = use ( "std:io" ); Quick Note: You can view module content using the dir function for undocumented components of the module. var io = use ( "std:io" ); echo dir ( io ); // [__name__, input, print, println]
docs.title: built-in modules

docs.text: std:fs : file system operation related functions and variables. std:io : module with functions for taking inputs and outputs to and from console. std:os : functions and classes handling OS and environment related operations. std:time : module with system clock and time related functions. std:util : utility functions for conversions, shortcuts and language features.
docs.title: index of std modules

docs.text: This module contains functions for interacting with the file system. Include using var fs = use ( "std:fs" ); read_file read_file ( path : String ) -> String Reads the contents of a file and returns it as a string. Parameters: path (String) : The path to the file to read. Returns: (String) The contents of the file. var fs = use ( "std:fs" ); var text = fs . read_file ( "hello.txt" ); write_file write_file ( path : String , content : Any ) -> Nil Writes data to a file. If the file already exists, its contents are replaced. Parameters: path (String) : The path to the target file. content (Any) : The data to write. Non-string types are converted to their string representations. Returns: (Nil) var fs = use ( "std:fs" ); fs . write_file ( "hello.txt" , "Hello, world!" ); exists exists ( path : String ) -> Bool Checks whether a file or directory exists. Parameters: path (String) : The path to check. Returns: (Bool) true if the path exists, otherwise false . var fs = use ( "std:fs" ); if ( fs . exists ( "hello.txt" )) { print ( "File exists!" ); } remove_file remove_file ( path : String ) -> Nil Deletes a file from the file system. Parameters: path (String) : The path to the file to be deleted. Returns: (Nil) var fs = use ( "std:fs" ); fs . remove_file ( "hello.txt" );
docs.title: fs

docs.text: read_file ( path : String ) -> String Reads the contents of a file and returns it as a string. Parameters: path (String) : The path to the file to read. Returns: (String) The contents of the file. var fs = use ( "std:fs" ); var text = fs . read_file ( "hello.txt" );
docs.title: read_file

docs.text: write_file ( path : String , content : Any ) -> Nil Writes data to a file. If the file already exists, its contents are replaced. Parameters: path (String) : The path to the target file. content (Any) : The data to write. Non-string types are converted to their string representations. Returns: (Nil) var fs = use ( "std:fs" ); fs . write_file ( "hello.txt" , "Hello, world!" );
docs.title: write_file

docs.text: exists ( path : String ) -> Bool Checks whether a file or directory exists. Parameters: path (String) : The path to check. Returns: (Bool) true if the path exists, otherwise false . var fs = use ( "std:fs" ); if ( fs . exists ( "hello.txt" )) { print ( "File exists!" ); }
docs.title: exists

docs.text: remove_file ( path : String ) -> Nil Deletes a file from the file system. Parameters: path (String) : The path to the file to be deleted. Returns: (Nil) var fs = use ( "std:fs" ); fs . remove_file ( "hello.txt" );
docs.title: remove_file

docs.text: This module contains functions related to input/output operations for communicating with the console. Include using var io = use ( "std:io" ); print print ( value : Any ) -> Nil Prints a value to the console. Parameters: value (Any) : The value to be printed to the output stream. Returns: (Nil) var io = use ( "std:io" ); io . print ( "hello, world! \n " ); println println ( value : Any ) -> Nil Same as print , but automatically appends a newline at the end. Parameters: value (Any) : The value to be printed to the output stream. Returns: (Nil) var io = use ( "std:io" ); io . println ( "hello, world!" ); input input ( prompt : String ) -> String Prompts the user for input and returns the entered text as a string. Parameters: prompt (String) : The text displayed to the user before waiting for input. Returns: (String) The text entered by the user. var io = use ( "std:io" ); var name = io . input ( "enter your name: " ); io . println ( "hello, ${name}!" );
docs.title: io

docs.text: print ( value : Any ) -> Nil Prints a value to the console. Parameters: value (Any) : The value to be printed to the output stream. Returns: (Nil) var io = use ( "std:io" ); io . print ( "hello, world! \n " );
docs.title: print

docs.text: println ( value : Any ) -> Nil Same as print , but automatically appends a newline at the end. Parameters: value (Any) : The value to be printed to the output stream. Returns: (Nil) var io = use ( "std:io" ); io . println ( "hello, world!" );
docs.title: println

docs.text: input ( prompt : String ) -> String Prompts the user for input and returns the entered text as a string. Parameters: prompt (String) : The text displayed to the user before waiting for input. Returns: (String) The text entered by the user. var io = use ( "std:io" ); var name = io . input ( "enter your name: " ); io . println ( "hello, ${name}!" );
docs.title: input

docs.text: This module contains functions for interacting with the operating system, command-line arguments, and environment variables. Include using var os = use ( "std:os" ); args args () -> List Retrieves the command-line arguments passed to the running process. Parameters: None Returns: (List) A list of strings containing the command-line arguments. var os = use ( "std:os" ); var arguments = os . args (); for ( var i = 0 ; i < arguments . len (); i = i + 1 ) { print ( arguments [ i ]); } load_dotenv load_dotenv ( path : String ) -> Nil Reads a .env file at the specified path and loads its contents into the current process's environment variables. Comments (lines starting with # ) and empty lines are ignored. Parameters: path (String) : The path to the .env file to read. Returns: (Nil) var os = use ( "std:os" ); os . load_dotenv ( ".env" ); get_env get_env ( key : String ) -> String | Nil Retrieves the value of an environment variable. Parameters: key (String) : The name of the environment variable. Returns: (String | Nil) The value of the environment variable as a string, or nil if it is not set. var os = use ( "std:os" ); var port = os . get_env ( "PORT" ); if ( port == nil ) { port = "8080" ; } print ( port ); set_env set_env ( key : String , value : String ) -> Nil Sets the value of an environment variable for the current process. Parameters: key (String) : The name of the environment variable. value (String) : The value to assign to the environment variable. Returns: (Nil) var os = use ( "std:os" ); os . set_env ( "DATABASE_URL" , "sqlite://dev.db" ); get_envs get_envs () -> List Retrieves all environment variables currently set in the process. Parameters: None Returns: (List) A list containing key-value pairs, where each pair is a nested list represented as [key: String, value: String] . var os = use ( "std:os" ); var envs = os . get_envs (); for ( var i = 0 ; i < envs . len (); i = i + 1 ) { var pair = envs [ i ]; var key = pair [ 0 ]; var val = pair [ 1 ]; print ( key + " = " + val ); } exit exit ( code : Number ) -> Nil Immediately terminates the current process with the specified exit status code. Parameters: code (Number) : The exit code status to return to the operating system. Returns: (Nil) var os = use ( "std:os" ); // Terminate with a non-zero status indicating an error os . exit ( 1 ); get_pid get_pid () -> Number Retrieves the Process ID (PID) of the current process. Parameters: None Returns: (Number) The numeric process identifier. var os = use ( "std:os" ); var pid = os . get_pid (); print ( "Running with PID: " + pid ); platform platform () -> String Returns a string representing the target operating system family (e.g., "windows" , "macos" , "linux" ). Parameters: None Returns: (String) The operating system name. var os = use ( "std:os" ); var current_platform = os . platform (); print ( "Host OS: " + current_platform ); arch arch () -> String Returns a string representing the host CPU architecture (e.g., "x86_64" , "aarch64" ). Parameters: None Returns: (String) The architecture name. var os = use ( "std:os" ); var architecture = os . arch (); print ( "Architecture: " + architecture ); current_dir current_dir () -> String Returns the current working directory path of the running process. Parameters: None Returns: (String) The absolute path of the current directory. var os = use ( "std:os" ); var cwd = os . current_dir (); print ( "Current directory: " + cwd ); set_current_dir set_current_dir ( path : String ) -> Nil Changes the current working directory of the process to the specified path. Parameters: path (String) : The directory path to switch to. Returns: (Nil) var os = use ( "std:os" ); os . set_current_dir ( "/var/tmp" ); execute execute ( command : String , args : List ) -> Dict Spawns a shell command or executable as a subprocess, blocks until execution completes, and returns its output streams along with the exit status code. Parameters: command (String) : The executable name or system command to run. args (List) : A list of string arguments to pass to the executable. Returns: (Dict) A dictionary containing the following keys: exit_code : (Number) The process exit status code (returns -1 if the process was terminated by a signal). stdout : (String) The captured stdout stream output. stderr : (String) The captured stderr stream output. var os = use ( "std:os" ); var result = os . execute ( "git" , [ "--version" ]); if ( result [ "exit_code" ] == 0 ) { print ( "Success: " + result [ "stdout" ]); } else { print ( "Failed: " + result [ "stderr" ]); }
docs.title: os

docs.text: args () -> List Retrieves the command-line arguments passed to the running process. Parameters: None Returns: (List) A list of strings containing the command-line arguments. var os = use ( "std:os" ); var arguments = os . args (); for ( var i = 0 ; i < arguments . len (); i = i + 1 ) { print ( arguments [ i ]); }
docs.title: args

docs.text: load_dotenv ( path : String ) -> Nil Reads a .env file at the specified path and loads its contents into the current process's environment variables. Comments (lines starting with # ) and empty lines are ignored. Parameters: path (String) : The path to the .env file to read. Returns: (Nil) var os = use ( "std:os" ); os . load_dotenv ( ".env" );
docs.title: load_dotenv

docs.text: get_env ( key : String ) -> String | Nil Retrieves the value of an environment variable. Parameters: key (String) : The name of the environment variable. Returns: (String | Nil) The value of the environment variable as a string, or nil if it is not set. var os = use ( "std:os" ); var port = os . get_env ( "PORT" ); if ( port == nil ) { port = "8080" ; } print ( port );
docs.title: get_env

docs.text: set_env ( key : String , value : String ) -> Nil Sets the value of an environment variable for the current process. Parameters: key (String) : The name of the environment variable. value (String) : The value to assign to the environment variable. Returns: (Nil) var os = use ( "std:os" ); os . set_env ( "DATABASE_URL" , "sqlite://dev.db" );
docs.title: set_env

docs.text: get_envs () -> List Retrieves all environment variables currently set in the process. Parameters: None Returns: (List) A list containing key-value pairs, where each pair is a nested list represented as [key: String, value: String] . var os = use ( "std:os" ); var envs = os . get_envs (); for ( var i = 0 ; i < envs . len (); i = i + 1 ) { var pair = envs [ i ]; var key = pair [ 0 ]; var val = pair [ 1 ]; print ( key + " = " + val ); }
docs.title: get_envs

docs.text: exit ( code : Number ) -> Nil Immediately terminates the current process with the specified exit status code. Parameters: code (Number) : The exit code status to return to the operating system. Returns: (Nil) var os = use ( "std:os" ); // Terminate with a non-zero status indicating an error os . exit ( 1 );
docs.title: exit

docs.text: get_pid () -> Number Retrieves the Process ID (PID) of the current process. Parameters: None Returns: (Number) The numeric process identifier. var os = use ( "std:os" ); var pid = os . get_pid (); print ( "Running with PID: " + pid );
docs.title: get_pid

docs.text: platform () -> String Returns a string representing the target operating system family (e.g., "windows" , "macos" , "linux" ). Parameters: None Returns: (String) The operating system name. var os = use ( "std:os" ); var current_platform = os . platform (); print ( "Host OS: " + current_platform );
docs.title: platform

docs.text: arch () -> String Returns a string representing the host CPU architecture (e.g., "x86_64" , "aarch64" ). Parameters: None Returns: (String) The architecture name. var os = use ( "std:os" ); var architecture = os . arch (); print ( "Architecture: " + architecture );

docs.text: current_dir () -> String Returns the current working directory path of the running process. Parameters: None Returns: (String) The absolute path of the current directory. var os = use ( "std:os" ); var cwd = os . current_dir (); print ( "Current directory: " + cwd );
docs.title: current_dir

docs.text: set_current_dir ( path : String ) -> Nil Changes the current working directory of the process to the specified path. Parameters: path (String) : The directory path to switch to. Returns: (Nil) var os = use ( "std:os" ); os . set_current_dir ( "/var/tmp" );
docs.title: set_current_dir

docs.text: execute ( command : String , args : List ) -> Dict Spawns a shell command or executable as a subprocess, blocks until execution completes, and returns its output streams along with the exit status code. Parameters: command (String) : The executable name or system command to run. args (List) : A list of string arguments to pass to the executable. Returns: (Dict) A dictionary containing the following keys: exit_code : (Number) The process exit status code (returns -1 if the process was terminated by a signal). stdout : (String) The captured stdout stream output. stderr : (String) The captured stderr stream output. var os = use ( "std:os" ); var result = os . execute ( "git" , [ "--version" ]); if ( result [ "exit_code" ] == 0 ) { print ( "Success: " + result [ "stdout" ]); } else { print ( "Failed: " + result [ "stderr" ]); }
docs.title: execute

docs.text: This module contains functions for working with time and date values. Include using var time = use ( "std:time" ); clock clock () -> Number Returns the current Unix timestamp as a number representing the number of seconds elapsed since January 1, 1970 (UTC). Parameters: None Returns: (Number) The current Unix timestamp in seconds. var time = use ( "std:time" ); var ts = time . clock (); print ( ts ); now now () -> Dict Returns the current date and time as a dictionary containing the fields year , month , day , hour , minute , and second . Parameters: None Returns: (Dict) A dictionary containing calendar and time components: year : The current year as a number. month : The current month (1-12) as a number. day : The current day of the month (1-31) as a number. hour : The current hour (0-23) as a number. minute : The current minute (0-59) as a number. second : The current second (0-59) as a number. var time = use ( "std:time" ); var now = time . now (); print ( now [ "year" ]); print ( now [ "month" ]); print ( now [ "day" ]); format format ( timestamp : Number , format : String ) -> String Formats a Unix timestamp using a format string. Supported format specifiers: %Y - four-digit year %m - two-digit month %d - two-digit day %H - two-digit hour %M - two-digit minute %S - two-digit second Parameters: timestamp (Number) : The Unix timestamp (in seconds) to format. format (String) : The template string containing the format specifiers. Returns: (String) The formatted date and time string. var time = use ( "std:time" ); var ts = time . clock (); var date = time . format ( ts , "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S" ); print ( date );
docs.title: time

docs.text: clock () -> Number Returns the current Unix timestamp as a number representing the number of seconds elapsed since January 1, 1970 (UTC). Parameters: None Returns: (Number) The current Unix timestamp in seconds. var time = use ( "std:time" ); var ts = time . clock (); print ( ts );
docs.title: clock

docs.text: now () -> Dict Returns the current date and time as a dictionary containing the fields year , month , day , hour , minute , and second . Parameters: None Returns: (Dict) A dictionary containing calendar and time components: year : The current year as a number. month : The current month (1-12) as a number. day : The current day of the month (1-31) as a number. hour : The current hour (0-23) as a number. minute : The current minute (0-59) as a number. second : The current second (0-59) as a number. var time = use ( "std:time" ); var now = time . now (); print ( now [ "year" ]); print ( now [ "month" ]); print ( now [ "day" ]);
docs.title: now

docs.text: format ( timestamp : Number , format : String ) -> String Formats a Unix timestamp using a format string. Supported format specifiers: %Y - four-digit year %m - two-digit month %d - two-digit day %H - two-digit hour %M - two-digit minute %S - two-digit second Parameters: timestamp (Number) : The Unix timestamp (in seconds) to format. format (String) : The template string containing the format specifiers. Returns: (String) The formatted date and time string. var time = use ( "std:time" ); var ts = time . clock (); var date = time . format ( ts , "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S" ); print ( date );
docs.title: format

docs.text: This module contains utility functions for conversions, shortcuts and language features. Include using var util = use ( "std:util" ); to_string to_string ( value : Any ) -> String Converts a value into its string representation. Parameters: value (Any) : The value to convert into a string. Returns: (String) The string representation of the given value. var util = use ( "std:util" ); var s = util . to_string ( 42 ); print ( s ); // "42" to_number to_number ( value : Any ) -> Number Converts a value into a number. Conversion rules: Number values are returned unchanged. String values are parsed as numbers. true converts to 1 . false converts to 0 . nil converts to 0 . Other types will raise a runtime error. Parameters: value (Any) : The value to parse or convert to a number. Returns: (Number) The numeric representation of the given value. var util = use ( "std:util" ); print ( util . to_number ( "123" )); // 123 print ( util . to_number ( true )); // 1 print ( util . to_number ( nil )); // 0 info info () -> Object Returns information about the current kyro runtime. The returned object contains the following fields: language version Parameters: None Returns: (Object) An object containing the runtime parameters: language : The string identifier of the language. version : The current version of the kyro interpreter. var util = use ( "std:util" ); var info = util . info (); print ( info . language ); print ( info . version );
docs.title: util

docs.text: to_string ( value : Any ) -> String Converts a value into its string representation. Parameters: value (Any) : The value to convert into a string. Returns: (String) The string representation of the given value. var util = use ( "std:util" ); var s = util . to_string ( 42 ); print ( s ); // "42"
docs.title: to_string

docs.text: to_number ( value : Any ) -> Number Converts a value into a number. Conversion rules: Number values are returned unchanged. String values are parsed as numbers. true converts to 1 . false converts to 0 . nil converts to 0 . Other types will raise a runtime error. Parameters: value (Any) : The value to parse or convert to a number. Returns: (Number) The numeric representation of the given value. var util = use ( "std:util" ); print ( util . to_number ( "123" )); // 123 print ( util . to_number ( true )); // 1 print ( util . to_number ( nil )); // 0
docs.title: to_number

docs.text: info () -> Object Returns information about the current kyro runtime. The returned object contains the following fields: language version Parameters: None Returns: (Object) An object containing the runtime parameters: language : The string identifier of the language. version : The current version of the kyro interpreter. var util = use ( "std:util" ); var info = util . info (); print ( info . language ); print ( info . version );
docs.title: info
